sections.paragraph:
κένωμα, ατος, τό (s. κενός; Philo Mech. 57, 17; 21; Polyb. 6, 31, 9; 11; Hero Alex. I 432, 5; Plut., Aemil. 266 [20, 8], Mor. 655b al.; PAmh 48, 8 [106 b.c.]; POxy 1292, 4 [c. 30 a.d.]; Aq.) empty space, emptiness τοῦ ἀνθρώπου of a person who does not possess the divine Spirit Hm 11:3.—DELG s.v. κενός.

Philo Mech Philo Mech , III–II b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

PAmh PAmh = The Amherst Papyri I–II—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 540
